stories.title: A Common Gut Bacterium, Reprogrammed, Is Helping Kill Cancer Tumors
stories.summary: Scientists have genetically engineered a common gut bacterium to slip past tumors' defenses and attack them from within, opening a promising new front against cancers that evade the immune system. It's a creative twist on immunotherapy that could broaden who benefits from it.

stories.title: Alzheimer's Fully Reversed in Mice With a Single Compound
stories.summary: Researchers using a compound called P7C3-A20 not only prevented Alzheimer's in genetically predisposed mice but actually restored memory and cognition in animals that already had the disease. The results raise real hope that human treatments could one day do more than just slow decline.

stories.title: Gene Therapy Gives People Born Deaf the Gift of Sound
stories.summary: A new gene therapy has produced what researchers are calling a life-changing breakthrough for people born with genetic hearing loss, restoring some hearing in patients for the first time. It's a literal sound of progress for a condition long thought untreatable.

stories.title: A Saliva Test at Home Could Catch Prostate Cancer Earlier
stories.summary: A new at-home saliva test has been developed that may identify prostate cancer sooner than current screenings, giving doctors a much better chance of treating it early. Researchers say it could 'turn the tide' on one of the most common cancers in men.

stories.title: 30,000 Danish Schoolkids Help Discover Nine New Tardigrade Species
stories.summary: A massive citizen-science project turned 30,000 Danish schoolchildren into field researchers, and their moss and leaf samples led to the discovery of nine new species of tardigrade — the nearly indestructible 'water bears.' Kids, microscopes, and tiny new creatures: science at its most charming.

stories.title: A Critically Endangered Antelope Is Back From the Brink
stories.summary: The Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative won a £1m global prize after its work saved a critically endangered antelope from extinction, one of several projects proving that focused conservation really can reverse the slide toward oblivion. It's a rare piece of extinction news with a happy ending.

stories.title: Renewables Just Passed a Milestone Scientists Call a 'Turning Point'
stories.summary: The world generated more than 30% of its electricity from renewables last year — a threshold analysts are calling a turning point in the history of energy. It means the shift away from fossil fuels is no longer theoretical; it's measurable, and it's accelerating.

stories.title: Indigenous Community Buys Back 10,000 Acres Near Lake Tahoe
stories.summary: An Indigenous community has purchased more than 10,000 acres of land near Lake Tahoe, California, for conservation — one of the largest tribal land returns in the state's history. It's a landmark moment for both ecological protection and restorative justice.

stories.title: The Ocean Cleanup Can Now Scrub a Football Field of Garbage Every Five Seconds
stories.summary: The Ocean Cleanup has launched 'System 03' in the Great Pacific Garbage Patch — nearly three times larger than its predecessor and capable of sweeping a football field's worth of plastic-laden ocean every five seconds. The scale of plastic removal is finally starting to match the scale of the problem.

stories.title: Hawaii Crews Pull 84 Tons of 'Ghost' Fishing Gear From the Pacific
stories.summary: Hawaii Pacific University's 'Bounty Project' has removed more than 185,000 pounds of derelict fishing gear from the North Pacific in just over three years, turning one of the ocean's deadliest forms of plastic trash into a solvable problem. Marine life — and beaches — are already noticing the difference.

stories.title: Locals Form a Human Bucket Brigade to Save a Stranded Whale
stories.summary: When a 15-foot minke whale became stranded on a beach, facing six hours of brutal sun and dehydration, locals organized themselves into a bucket brigade to keep the animal alive until high tide. It's a vivid reminder of what strangers will do for a life that isn't even their own species.

stories.title: A Collie Falls 100 Feet Off a Cliff — and Trots Away Unharmed
stories.summary: Whisp the collie plunged 100 feet down a sheer cliff on Scotland's Shetland Islands, prompting a full team rescue operation. Astonishingly, he was returned to his owner 'remarkably unscathed' and, by the look of the rescue photo, in excellent spirits.

stories.title: Two Dogs and a Cat, Rescued Together After Surviving the Streets as a Trio
stories.summary: River, Rain, and Reed — two dogs and a cat — were found wandering the streets of Vermont late at night, having clearly been sticking together for survival. The Central Vermont Humane Society took them in as a 'real-life Homeward Bound,' and their loyalty to one another melted hearts online.

stories.title: Hero Dog Figures Out the Doorknob to Save His Grandpa's Life
stories.summary: Kai, a dog never trained to open doors, somehow worked out a round doorknob on his own, got outside, and barked until neighbors came running — saving his grandfather during a medical emergency. It's the kind of quick thinking humans get medals for.

stories.title: At 90, She's Rescued 10,000 Animals — and She's Not Done Yet
stories.summary: Barby Keel has spent 54 years running an animal sanctuary and is credited with rescuing an estimated 10,000 animals from neglect and abuse. At 90 years old, she flatly refuses to retire, which is exactly the energy this planet needs.

stories.title: A Welder's Split-Second Decision on the Highway Saved a Stranger's Life
stories.summary: When a driver suffered a medical emergency behind the wheel, a welder named Villarreal-Albe used his truck to help stop the vehicle and then rushed to pull the man to safety — aided, remarkably, by a nurse who happened to be passing at exactly the right moment. Two strangers, one life saved.

stories.title: Family of Seven 'Adopts' Their 82-Year-Old Widower Neighbor as Grandpa
stories.summary: Moved by their widowed 82-year-old neighbor's kindness, a family of seven unofficially adopted him — inviting him to stay over, sharing meals, and letting their kids treat him like a real grandfather. 'The kids run up to him like that's their grandfather,' the mom said, and honestly, now he is.

stories.title: A Teen Cut Off His 19-Inch Afro and Raised $38,000 for Kids With Cancer
stories.summary: Kieran Moïse had spent years growing a spectacular 19-inch afro — and then cut the whole thing off, turning the moment into a fundraiser that brought in $38,000 for children battling cancer. Hair grows back; that donation is going to matter forever.

stories.title: A 13-Year-Old Raised $6,000 to Give Kids in a Homeless Shelter a Real Christmas
stories.summary: Jaxson Turner, just 13, raised nearly $6,000 through GoFundMe and social media to buy every child at The Samaritan Inn homeless shelter new sneakers, coats, socks, toys — and a hot holiday meal. A one-kid operation that out-cheered most of us this Christmas.

stories.title: Old Route 66 Motels Are Becoming Homes for People Who Need Them
stories.summary: In Albuquerque, developer Chavez is converting faded Route 66 motels like the Adobe Manor into genuine affordable housing, rescuing both the historic buildings and the residents who were living in them under rough conditions. It's a clever fix for two crises at once: vanishing Americana and the housing shortage.
